content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Unincorporated Marin | Marin County
\
  - url: https://www.marincounty.gov/departments/cda/building-and-safety/how-apply-building-permit/about-building-permits/unincorporated-marin
\
  - source: County of Marin
- domain: marincounty.gov

## Content

# Unincorporated\
  \ Marin

What is unincorporated Marin, and why does it matter?

Marin County\
  \ explains that unincorporated Marin means a place without its own mayor and police\
  \ department. These places are served by county government. The County states that\
  \ it helps with police, roads, and building permits for unincorporated places.
\
  
If you live in an incorporated city, the County directs you to contact the city\
  \ government that provides those services.

The page points residents to a property\
  \ jurisdiction information tool. That tool tells whether an address is part of an\
  \ incorporated city or part of unincorporated Marin. The lookup can use an address\
  \ or a parcel number.

Communities listed on the County page as unincorporated\
  \ Marin include Black Point, Bolinas, Dillon Beach, Forest Knolls, Ignacio, Inverness,\
  \ Kentfield, Lagunitas, Lucas Valley, Marin City, Marinwood, Nicasio, Paradise Cay,\
  \ San Quentin, St. Vincent’s, Strawberry, Tamalpais Valley, Tocaloma, and Woodacre.
\
  
Related links on the page include the Community Development Agency and Property\
  \ jurisdiction lookup.

Contact information on the page is for Building and Safety\
  \ General Contact: 415-473-6550. The listed office address is 3501 Civic Center\
  \ Drive, Suite 308, San Rafael, CA 94903. Office hours shown are Monday through\
  \ Thursday 8:00 am to 4:00 pm, and Friday 8:00 am to 12:00 pm.

Page last updated\
  \ on December 31, 2025.
"
metadata: {}
title: Unincorporated Marin | Marin County
url: https://www.marincounty.gov/departments/cda/building-and-safety/how-apply-building-permit/about-building-permits/unincorporated-marin

Your property is in **unincorporated Marin County**, even though its mailing locality is San Rafael. Therefore, you should start with:

**Marin County Community Development Agency — Building and Safety**  
3501 Civic Center Drive, Suite 308  
San Rafael, CA 94903  
**Phone:** 415-473-6550  
**Hours:** Monday–Thursday, 8:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m.; Friday, 8:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m.

A deck addition generally requires a building permit, so use the **Marin County** permit process—not the City of San Rafael’s building-permit office. The county’s official information is available at:  
https://www.marincounty.gov/departments/cda/building-and-safety/how-apply-building-permit/about-building-permits/unincorporated-marin

When contacting them, describe the project as a **residential deck addition** and ask about required plans, structural details, setbacks, and any planning or environmental review.

Your property is in **unincorporated Marin County**, even though its mailing locality is San Rafael. Therefore, you should start with:

**Marin County Community Development Agency — Building and Safety**  
3501 Civic Center Drive, Suite 308  
San Rafael, CA 94903  
**Phone:** 415-473-6550  
**Hours:** Monday–Thursday, 8:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m.; Friday, 8:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m.

A deck addition generally requires a building permit, so use the **Marin County** permit process—not the City of San Rafael’s building-permit office. The county’s official information is available at:  
https://www.marincounty.gov/departments/cda/building-and-safety/how-apply-building-permit/about-building-permits/unincorporated-marin

When contacting them, describe the project as a **residential deck addition** and ask about required plans, structural details, setbacks, and any planning or environmental review.
